LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS BALDWIN YUNG AND WORLDWIDE CAPITAL ASIA CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD MORE FACTS TO SUPPORT THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS. THE REMAINING GROUNDS FOR DEMURRER ARE OVERRULED. NONE OF THE ALLEGED CONTRACTS REQUIRES CONSUMMATION OF A HOTEL TRANSACTION AS A CONDITION PRECEDENT FOR PAYMENT. IN RULING ON A DEMURRER, THE COURT ACCEPTS PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTRACT PURSUANT TO ARAGON-HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES, INC., (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 232, 239. THE COURT ALSO REJECTS THE AGREEMENT TO AGREE ARGUMENT. THE MOU DOES NOT FACIALLY DISCLOSE AN AGREEMENT TO AGREE. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT REGARDING A BROKER'S LICENSE. PLAINTIFF IS NO OBLIGATED TO PLEAD AROUND THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE OF ILLEGALITY AND A FACTUAL ISSUE REMAINS REGARDING WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HAD THE REQUIRED LICENSE OR ENGAGED IN NEGOTIATIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Indispensable Party Within Three Years
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Steiner of Law Offices of Paul J. Steiner telephone number 415-981-6100 for Plaintiff Robert Icho.
Malcolm Leader-Picone of Bartlett, Leader-Picone & Young, LLP telephone number 510-444-2404 appeared by telephone for Defendant Baldwin Bok Yung.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BALDWIN YUNG and WORLDWIDE CAPITAL ASIA CORPORATION'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Indispensable Party Within Three Years is denied. Defendants have not cited any authority for the proposition that failure to serve any defendant within three years requires dismissal of all defendants. Neither the relevant statutes nor Perati v. Atkinson (1964) 230 Cal. App. 2d 251 require a dismissal of defendants who have been served within three years. Nor have defendants cited any authority for the proposition that service of a complaint, if it is the operative pleading at the time, is insufficient to satisfy the obligation to effect service within three years when a plaintiff subsequently files an amended complaint. Nor have defendants shown that, even assuming that Wo Cheung and Kai Yin are necessary parties per CCP 389(a), equity and good conscience requires dismissal of the case as to all defendants rather than proceeding in the absence of those parties per CCP 389(b). Consideration of the factors stated in 389(b) favor proceeding with this case in the absence of Wo Cheung and Kai Yin. Order signed in open Court.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Icho, Tel. 510.910.0292, plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS CHERIE LAU, BOAZ YUNG, and BALDWIN YUNG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled and the Motion to Strike is denied. Ownership of stock is not a prerequisite for the application of the alter ego doctrine. (See Riddle v. Leuschner (1959) 51 Cal.2d 574 [finding defendant was the alter ego of one corporation in which she held no stock].) Rather, the analysis focuses on the question of control. (See, e.g., Sonora Diamond Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 523, 538 ["the courts will ignore the corporate entity and deem the corporation's acts to be those of the persons or organizations actually controlling the corporation, in most instances the equitable owners"].) Additionally, Defendants' contention that the First Amended Complaint lacks allegations of wrongdoing that could amount to the imposition of liability against Defendants Lau and Boaz Yung fails in light of dozens of such allegations contained in the pleading. (See, e.g., Dem. 11, 13, 14, 17, 18, 20(A) and (G), 21(A) and (G), 22(A) and (G).) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs BALDWIN YUNG and WORLDWIDE CAPITAL ASIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED with 15 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead more facts to support the alter ego allegations. The remaining grounds for demurrer are overruled. None of the alleged contracts requires consummation of a hotel transaction as a condition precedent for payment. In ruling on a demurrer, the Court accepts plaintiff's interpretation of the contract pursuant to Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239. The Court also rejects the agreement to agree argument. The MOU does not facially disclose an agreement to agree. The Court rejects defendant's argument regarding a broker's license. Plaintiff is no obligated to plead around the affirmative defense of illegality and a factual issue remains regarding whether the plaintiff had the required license or engaged in negotiations. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
